case_title: CELIA GONZALES VS. CALIFORNIA STATE AUTOMOBILE ASSOCIATION, A et al

docket_entries.description: LAW AND MOTION 301, NO APPEARANCES ON THIS MATTER. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: DEFENDANT CALIFORNIA STATE AUTOMOBILE ASSOCIATION'S DEMURRER COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO CAUSE OF ACTION 5. AS ANY RELEVANT DOCUMENTS ARE WITHHELD PURSUANT TO A CLAIM OF PRIVILEGE OR PRIVACY, THEY MUST BE IDENTIFIED WITH PARTICULARITY. PLAINTIFF'S REQUEST FOR SANCTIONS IS DENIED. COMMISSIONER EVERETT A. LAW AND MOTION 301, RULING - A R G U E D; THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: PLAINTIFF/CROSS-DEFENDANT CELIA GONZALES' SPECIAL MOTION TO STRIKE (C C P 425.16) THE CROSS-COMPLAINT OF LITTAU HARVESTER, INCORPORATED IS DENIED. THE COMPLAINT DOES NOT ARISE FROM PROTECTED CONDUCT SINCE IT IS BASED ON CROSS-DEFENDANT BREACHING THE AGREEMENT BY FAILING TO TAKE STEPS NOT TO SEEK DOUBLE RECOVERY. IN ANY EVENT, CROSS-COMPLAINANT HAS SHOWN A SUFFICIENT LIKELIHOOD OF SUCCESS BY OFFERING A REASONABLE INTERPRETATION OF PARTIES AGREEMENT THAT, IF ACCEPTED BY THE TRIER OF FACT, WOULD HAVE BEEN BREACH BY CROSS-DEFENDANT'S FAILURE TO TAKE SUCH STEPS. MR. WELCH TO SUBMIT A PROPOSED FORM OF ORDER. JUDGE: PETER J. BUSCH, REPORTER: CAROL KAREN, CSR # 8189 301/PJB

Off Calendar Oct-07-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. FELIPE R. PARKER, TEL. NO. (510) 545-4980 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF/CROSS DEFENDANT;
ATTY. MICHAEL R. WELCH, TEL. NO. (510) 981-1698 APPEARING FOR THE CROSS-COMPLAINANT LITTAU HARVESTER.

RULING - A R G U E D; THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: PLAINTIFF/CROSS-DEFENDANT CELIA GONZALES' SPECIAL Motion To Strike (C C P 425.16) The CROSS-Complaint Of Littau Harvester, Incorporated IS DENIED. THE COMPLAINT DOES NOT ARISE FROM PROTECTED CONDUCT SINCE IT IS BASED ON CROSS-DEFENDANT BREACHING THE AGREEMENT BY FAILING TO TAKE STEPS NOT TO SEEK DOUBLE RECOVERY. IN ANY EVENT, CROSS-COMPLAINANT HAS SHOWN A SUFFICIENT LIKELIHOOD OF SUCCESS BY OFFERING A REASONABLE INTERPRETATION OF PARTIES AGREEMENT THAT, IF ACCEPTED BY THE TRIER OF FACT, WOULD HAVE BEEN BREACH BY CROSS-DEFENDANT'S FAILURE TO TAKE SUCH STEPS. Mr. Welch to submit a proposed form of order. Judge: Peter J. Busch, Reporter: Carol Karen, CSR # 8189 =301/PJB
